properties.name: management.appoptics.metrics.export.api-token
properties.description: AppOptics API token.

properties.name: management.appoptics.metrics.export.batch-size
properties.description: Number of measurements per request to use for this backend. If more measurements are found, then multiple requests will be made.

properties.name: management.appoptics.metrics.export.connect-timeout
properties.description: Connection timeout for requests to this backend.

properties.name: management.appoptics.metrics.export.enabled
properties.description: Whether exporting of metrics to this backend is enabled.

properties.name: management.appoptics.metrics.export.floor-times
properties.description: Whether to ship a floored time, useful when sending measurements from multiple hosts to align them on a given time boundary.

properties.name: management.appoptics.metrics.export.host-tag
properties.description: Tag that will be mapped to "@host" when shipping metrics to AppOptics.

properties.name: management.appoptics.metrics.export.read-timeout
properties.description: Read timeout for requests to this backend.

properties.name: management.appoptics.metrics.export.step
properties.description: Step size (i.e. reporting frequency) to use.

properties.name: management.appoptics.metrics.export.uri
properties.description: URI to ship metrics to.

properties.name: management.atlas.metrics.export.batch-size
properties.description: Number of measurements per request to use for this backend. If more measurements are found, then multiple requests will be made.

properties.name: management.atlas.metrics.export.config-refresh-frequency
properties.description: Frequency for refreshing config settings from the LWC service.

properties.name: management.atlas.metrics.export.config-time-to-live
properties.description: Time to live for subscriptions from the LWC service.

properties.name: management.atlas.metrics.export.config-uri
properties.description: URI for the Atlas LWC endpoint to retrieve current subscriptions.

properties.name: management.atlas.metrics.export.connect-timeout
properties.description: Connection timeout for requests to this backend.

properties.name: management.atlas.metrics.export.enabled
properties.description: Whether exporting of metrics to this backend is enabled.

properties.name: management.atlas.metrics.export.eval-uri
properties.description: URI for the Atlas LWC endpoint to evaluate the data for a subscription.

properties.name: management.atlas.metrics.export.lwc-enabled
properties.description: Whether to enable streaming to Atlas LWC.

properties.name: management.atlas.metrics.export.lwc-ignore-publish-step
properties.description: Whether expressions with the same step size as Atlas publishing should be ignored for streaming. Used for cases where data being published to Atlas is also sent into streaming from the backend.

properties.name: management.atlas.metrics.export.lwc-step
properties.description: Step size (reporting frequency) to use for streaming to Atlas LWC. This is the highest supported resolution for getting an on-demand stream of the data. It must be less than or equal to management.metrics.export.atlas.step and management.metrics.export.atlas.step should be an even multiple of this value.

properties.name: management.atlas.metrics.export.meter-time-to-live
properties.description: Time to live for meters that do not have any activity. After this period the meter will be considered expired and will not get reported.

properties.name: management.atlas.metrics.export.num-threads
properties.description: Number of threads to use with the metrics publishing scheduler.

properties.name: management.atlas.metrics.export.read-timeout
properties.description: Read timeout for requests to this backend.

properties.name: management.atlas.metrics.export.step
properties.description: Step size (i.e. reporting frequency) to use.

properties.name: management.atlas.metrics.export.uri
properties.description: URI of the Atlas server.

properties.name: management.datadog.metrics.export.api-key
properties.description: Datadog API key.

properties.name: management.datadog.metrics.export.application-key
properties.description: Datadog application key. Not strictly required, but improves the Datadog experience by sending meter descriptions, types, and base units to Datadog.

properties.name: management.datadog.metrics.export.batch-size
properties.description: Number of measurements per request to use for this backend. If more measurements are found, then multiple requests will be made.

properties.name: management.datadog.metrics.export.connect-timeout
properties.description: Connection timeout for requests to this backend.

properties.name: management.datadog.metrics.export.descriptions
properties.description: Whether to publish descriptions metadata to Datadog. Turn this off to minimize the amount of metadata sent.

properties.name: management.datadog.metrics.export.enabled
properties.description: Whether exporting of metrics to this backend is enabled.

properties.name: management.datadog.metrics.export.host-tag
properties.description: Tag that will be mapped to "host" when shipping metrics to Datadog.

properties.name: management.datadog.metrics.export.read-timeout
properties.description: Read timeout for requests to this backend.

properties.name: management.datadog.metrics.export.step
properties.description: Step size (i.e. reporting frequency) to use.

properties.name: management.datadog.metrics.export.uri
properties.description: URI to ship metrics to. Set this if you need to publish metrics to a Datadog site other than US, or to an internal proxy en-route to Datadog.

properties.name: management.defaults.metrics.export.enabled
properties.description: Whether to enable default metrics exporters.

properties.name: management.dynatrace.metrics.export.api-token
properties.description: Dynatrace authentication token.

properties.name: management.dynatrace.metrics.export.batch-size
properties.description: Number of measurements per request to use for this backend. If more measurements are found, then multiple requests will be made.

properties.name: management.dynatrace.metrics.export.connect-timeout
properties.description: Connection timeout for requests to this backend.

properties.name: management.dynatrace.metrics.export.enabled
properties.description: Whether exporting of metrics to this backend is enabled.

properties.name: management.dynatrace.metrics.export.read-timeout
properties.description: Read timeout for requests to this backend.

properties.name: management.dynatrace.metrics.export.step
properties.description: Step size (i.e. reporting frequency) to use.

properties.name: management.dynatrace.metrics.export.uri
properties.description: URI to ship metrics to. Should be used for SaaS, self-managed instances or to en-route through an internal proxy.

properties.name: management.dynatrace.metrics.export.v1.device-id
properties.description: ID of the custom device that is exporting metrics to Dynatrace.

properties.name: management.dynatrace.metrics.export.v1.group
properties.description: Group for exported metrics. Used to specify custom device group name in the Dynatrace UI.

properties.name: management.dynatrace.metrics.export.v1.technology-type
properties.description: Technology type for exported metrics. Used to group metrics under a logical technology name in the Dynatrace UI.

properties.name: management.dynatrace.metrics.export.v2.default-dimensions
properties.description: Default dimensions that are added to all metrics in the form of key-value pairs. These are overwritten by Micrometer tags if they use the same key.

properties.name: management.dynatrace.metrics.export.v2.enrich-with-dynatrace-metadata
properties.description: Whether to enable Dynatrace metadata export.

properties.name: management.dynatrace.metrics.export.v2.export-meter-metadata
properties.description: Whether to export meter metadata (unit and description) to the Dynatrace backend.

properties.name: management.dynatrace.metrics.export.v2.metric-key-prefix
properties.description: Prefix string that is added to all exported metrics.

properties.name: management.dynatrace.metrics.export.v2.use-dynatrace-summary-instruments
properties.description: Whether to fall back to the built-in micrometer instruments for Timer and DistributionSummary.

properties.name: management.elastic.metrics.export.api-key-credentials
properties.description: Base64-encoded credentials string. Mutually exclusive with user-name and password.

properties.name: management.elastic.metrics.export.auto-create-index
properties.description: Whether to create the index automatically if it does not exist.

properties.name: management.elastic.metrics.export.batch-size
properties.description: Number of measurements per request to use for this backend. If more measurements are found, then multiple requests will be made.

properties.name: management.elastic.metrics.export.connect-timeout
properties.description: Connection timeout for requests to this backend.

properties.name: management.elastic.metrics.export.enable-source
properties.description: Whether to enable _source in the default index template when auto-creating the index.

properties.name: management.elastic.metrics.export.enabled
properties.description: Whether exporting of metrics to this backend is enabled.

properties.name: management.elastic.metrics.export.host
properties.description: Host to export metrics to.

properties.name: management.elastic.metrics.export.index
properties.description: Index to export metrics to.

properties.name: management.elastic.metrics.export.index-date-format
properties.description: Index date format used for rolling indices. Appended to the index name.

properties.name: management.elastic.metrics.export.index-date-separator
properties.description: Prefix to separate the index name from the date format used for rolling indices.

properties.name: management.elastic.metrics.export.password
properties.description: Login password of the Elastic server. Mutually exclusive with api-key-credentials.

properties.name: management.elastic.metrics.export.pipeline
properties.description: Ingest pipeline name. By default, events are not pre-processed.

properties.name: management.elastic.metrics.export.read-timeout
properties.description: Read timeout for requests to this backend.

properties.name: management.elastic.metrics.export.step
properties.description: Step size (i.e. reporting frequency) to use.

properties.name: management.elastic.metrics.export.timestamp-field-name
properties.description: Name of the timestamp field.

properties.name: management.elastic.metrics.export.user-name
properties.description: Login user of the Elastic server. Mutually exclusive with api-key-credentials.

properties.name: management.endpoint.metrics.access
properties.description: Permitted level of access for the metrics endpoint.

properties.name: management.endpoint.metrics.cache.time-to-live
properties.description: Maximum time that a response can be cached.

properties.name: management.endpoint.prometheus.access
properties.description: Permitted level of access for the prometheus endpoint.

properties.name: management.ganglia.metrics.export.addressing-mode
properties.description: UDP addressing mode, either unicast or multicast.

properties.name: management.ganglia.metrics.export.duration-units
properties.description: Base time unit used to report durations.

properties.name: management.ganglia.metrics.export.enabled
properties.description: Whether exporting of metrics to Ganglia is enabled.

properties.name: management.ganglia.metrics.export.host
properties.description: Host of the Ganglia server to receive exported metrics.

properties.name: management.ganglia.metrics.export.port
properties.description: Port of the Ganglia server to receive exported metrics.

properties.name: management.ganglia.metrics.export.step
properties.description: Step size (i.e. reporting frequency) to use.

properties.name: management.ganglia.metrics.export.time-to-live
properties.description: Time to live for metrics on Ganglia. Set the multicast Time-To-Live to be one greater than the number of hops (routers) between the hosts.

properties.name: management.graphite.metrics.export.duration-units
properties.description: Base time unit used to report durations.

properties.name: management.graphite.metrics.export.enabled
properties.description: Whether exporting of metrics to Graphite is enabled.

properties.name: management.graphite.metrics.export.graphite-tags-enabled
properties.description: Whether Graphite tags should be used, as opposed to a hierarchical naming convention. Enabled by default unless "tagsAsPrefix" is set.

properties.name: management.graphite.metrics.export.host
properties.description: Host of the Graphite server to receive exported metrics.

properties.name: management.graphite.metrics.export.port
properties.description: Port of the Graphite server to receive exported metrics.

properties.name: management.graphite.metrics.export.protocol
properties.description: Protocol to use while shipping data to Graphite.

properties.name: management.graphite.metrics.export.rate-units
properties.description: Base time unit used to report rates.

properties.name: management.graphite.metrics.export.step
properties.description: Step size (i.e. reporting frequency) to use.

properties.name: management.graphite.metrics.export.tags-as-prefix
properties.description: For the hierarchical naming convention, turn the specified tag keys into part of the metric prefix. Ignored if "graphiteTagsEnabled" is true.

properties.name: management.humio.metrics.export.api-token
properties.description: Humio API token.

properties.name: management.humio.metrics.export.batch-size
properties.description: Number of measurements per request to use for this backend. If more measurements are found, then multiple requests will be made.

properties.name: management.humio.metrics.export.connect-timeout
properties.description: Connection timeout for requests to this backend.

properties.name: management.humio.metrics.export.enabled
properties.description: Whether exporting of metrics to this backend is enabled.

properties.name: management.humio.metrics.export.read-timeout
properties.description: Read timeout for requests to this backend.

properties.name: management.humio.metrics.export.step
properties.description: Step size (i.e. reporting frequency) to use.

properties.name: management.humio.metrics.export.tags
properties.description: Humio tags describing the data source in which metrics will be stored. Humio tags are a distinct concept from Micrometer's tags. Micrometer's tags are used to divide metrics along dimensional boundaries.

properties.name: management.humio.metrics.export.uri
properties.description: URI to ship metrics to. If you need to publish metrics to an internal proxy en-route to Humio, you can define the location of the proxy with this.

properties.name: management.influx.metrics.export.api-version
properties.description: API version of InfluxDB to use. Defaults to 'v1' unless an org is configured. If an org is configured, defaults to 'v2'.

properties.name: management.influx.metrics.export.auto-create-db
properties.description: Whether to create the Influx database if it does not exist before attempting to publish metrics to it. InfluxDB v1 only.

properties.name: management.influx.metrics.export.batch-size
properties.description: Number of measurements per request to use for this backend. If more measurements are found, then multiple requests will be made.

properties.name: management.influx.metrics.export.bucket
properties.description: Bucket for metrics. Use either the bucket name or ID. Defaults to the value of the db property if not set. InfluxDB v2 only.

properties.name: management.influx.metrics.export.compressed
properties.description: Whether to enable GZIP compression of metrics batches published to Influx.

properties.name: management.influx.metrics.export.connect-timeout
properties.description: Connection timeout for requests to this backend.

properties.name: management.influx.metrics.export.consistency
properties.description: Write consistency for each point.

properties.name: management.influx.metrics.export.db
properties.description: Database to send metrics to. InfluxDB v1 only.

properties.name: management.influx.metrics.export.enabled
properties.description: Whether exporting of metrics to this backend is enabled.

properties.name: management.influx.metrics.export.org
properties.description: Org to write metrics to. InfluxDB v2 only.

properties.name: management.influx.metrics.export.password
properties.description: Login password of the Influx server. InfluxDB v1 only.

properties.name: management.influx.metrics.export.read-timeout
properties.description: Read timeout for requests to this backend.

properties.name: management.influx.metrics.export.retention-duration
properties.description: Time period for which Influx should retain data in the current database. For instance 7d, check the influx documentation for more details on the duration format. InfluxDB v1 only.

properties.name: management.influx.metrics.export.retention-policy
properties.description: Retention policy to use (Influx writes to the DEFAULT retention policy if one is not specified). InfluxDB v1 only.

properties.name: management.influx.metrics.export.retention-replication-factor
properties.description: How many copies of the data are stored in the cluster. Must be 1 for a single node instance. InfluxDB v1 only.

properties.name: management.influx.metrics.export.retention-shard-duration
properties.description: Time range covered by a shard group. For instance 2w, check the influx documentation for more details on the duration format. InfluxDB v1 only.

properties.name: management.influx.metrics.export.step
properties.description: Step size (i.e. reporting frequency) to use.

properties.name: management.influx.metrics.export.token
properties.description: Authentication token to use with calls to the InfluxDB backend. For InfluxDB v1, the Bearer scheme is used. For v2, the Token scheme is used.

properties.name: management.influx.metrics.export.uri
properties.description: URI of the Influx server.

properties.name: management.influx.metrics.export.user-name
properties.description: Login user of the Influx server. InfluxDB v1 only.

properties.name: management.jmx.metrics.export.domain
properties.description: Metrics JMX domain name.

properties.name: management.jmx.metrics.export.enabled
properties.description: Whether exporting of metrics to this backend is enabled.

properties.name: management.jmx.metrics.export.step
properties.description: Step size (i.e. reporting frequency) to use.

properties.name: management.kairos.metrics.export.batch-size
properties.description: Number of measurements per request to use for this backend. If more measurements are found, then multiple requests will be made.

properties.name: management.kairos.metrics.export.connect-timeout
properties.description: Connection timeout for requests to this backend.

properties.name: management.kairos.metrics.export.enabled
properties.description: Whether exporting of metrics to this backend is enabled.

properties.name: management.kairos.metrics.export.password
properties.description: Login password of the KairosDB server.

properties.name: management.kairos.metrics.export.read-timeout
properties.description: Read timeout for requests to this backend.

properties.name: management.kairos.metrics.export.step
properties.description: Step size (i.e. reporting frequency) to use.

properties.name: management.kairos.metrics.export.uri
properties.description: URI of the KairosDB server.

properties.name: management.kairos.metrics.export.user-name
properties.description: Login user of the KairosDB server.

properties.name: management.metrics.distribution.buffer-length
properties.description: Number of histograms for meter IDs starting with the specified name to keep in the ring buffer. The longest match wins, the key `all` can also be used to configure all meters.

properties.name: management.metrics.distribution.expiry
properties.description: Maximum amount of time that samples for meter IDs starting with the specified name are accumulated to decaying distribution statistics before they are reset and rotated. The longest match wins, the key `all` can also be used to configure all meters.

properties.name: management.metrics.distribution.maximum-expected-value
properties.description: Maximum value that meter IDs starting with the specified name are expected to observe. The longest match wins. Values can be specified as a double or as a Duration value (for timer meters, defaulting to ms if no unit specified).

properties.name: management.metrics.distribution.minimum-expected-value
properties.description: Minimum value that meter IDs starting with the specified name are expected to observe. The longest match wins. Values can be specified as a double or as a Duration value (for timer meters, defaulting to ms if no unit specified).

properties.name: management.metrics.distribution.percentiles
properties.description: Specific computed non-aggregable percentiles to ship to the backend for meter IDs starting-with the specified name. The longest match wins, the key 'all' can also be used to configure all meters.

properties.name: management.metrics.distribution.percentiles-histogram
properties.description: Whether meter IDs starting with the specified name should publish percentile histograms. For monitoring systems that support aggregable percentile calculation based on a histogram, this can be set to true. For other systems, this has no effect. The longest match wins, the key 'all' can also be used to configure all meters.

properties.name: management.metrics.distribution.slo
properties.description: Specific service-level objective boundaries for meter IDs starting with the specified name. The longest match wins. Counters will be published for each specified boundary. Values can be specified as a double or as a Duration value (for timer meters, defaulting to ms if no unit specified).

properties.name: management.metrics.enable
properties.description: Whether meter IDs starting with the specified name should be enabled. The longest match wins, the key 'all' can also be used to configure all meters.

properties.name: management.metrics.observations.ignored-meters
properties.description: Meters that should be ignored when recoding observations.

properties.name: management.metrics.system.diskspace.paths
properties.description: List of paths to report disk metrics for.

properties.name: management.metrics.tags
properties.description: Common tags that are applied to every meter.

properties.name: management.metrics.use-global-registry
properties.description: Whether auto-configured MeterRegistry implementations should be bound to the global static registry on Metrics. For testing, set this to 'false' to maximize test independence.

properties.name: management.metrics.web.client.max-uri-tags
properties.description: Maximum number of unique URI tag values allowed. After the max number of tag values is reached, metrics with additional tag values are denied by filter.

properties.name: management.metrics.web.server.max-uri-tags
properties.description: Maximum number of unique URI tag values allowed. After the max number of tag values is reached, metrics with additional tag values are denied by filter.

properties.name: management.newrelic.metrics.export.account-id
properties.description: New Relic account ID.

properties.name: management.newrelic.metrics.export.api-key
properties.description: New Relic API key.

properties.name: management.newrelic.metrics.export.batch-size
properties.description: Number of measurements per request to use for this backend. If more measurements are found, then multiple requests will be made.

properties.name: management.newrelic.metrics.export.client-provider-type
properties.description: Client provider type to use.

properties.name: management.newrelic.metrics.export.connect-timeout
properties.description: Connection timeout for requests to this backend.

properties.name: management.newrelic.metrics.export.enabled
properties.description: Whether exporting of metrics to this backend is enabled.

properties.name: management.newrelic.metrics.export.event-type
properties.description: The event type that should be published. This property will be ignored if 'meter-name-event-type-enabled' is set to 'true'.

properties.name: management.newrelic.metrics.export.meter-name-event-type-enabled
properties.description: Whether to send the meter name as the event type instead of using the 'event-type' configuration property value. Can be set to 'true' if New Relic guidelines are not being followed or event types consistent with previous Spring Boot releases are required.

properties.name: management.newrelic.metrics.export.read-timeout
properties.description: Read timeout for requests to this backend.

properties.name: management.newrelic.metrics.export.step
properties.description: Step size (i.e. reporting frequency) to use.

properties.name: management.newrelic.metrics.export.uri
properties.description: URI to ship metrics to.

properties.name: management.otlp.metrics.export.aggregation-temporality
properties.description: Aggregation temporality of sums. It defines the way additive values are expressed. This setting depends on the backend you use, some only support one temporality.

properties.name: management.otlp.metrics.export.base-time-unit
properties.description: Time unit for exported metrics.

properties.name: management.otlp.metrics.export.batch-size
properties.description: Number of measurements per request to use for this backend. If more measurements are found, then multiple requests will be made.

properties.name: management.otlp.metrics.export.connect-timeout
properties.description: Connection timeout for requests to this backend.

properties.name: management.otlp.metrics.export.enabled
properties.description: Whether exporting of metrics to this backend is enabled.

properties.name: management.otlp.metrics.export.headers
properties.description: Headers for the exported metrics.

properties.name: management.otlp.metrics.export.histogram-flavor
properties.description: Default histogram type when histogram publishing is enabled.

properties.name: management.otlp.metrics.export.max-bucket-count
properties.description: Default maximum number of buckets to be used for exponential histograms, if configured. This has no effect on explicit bucket histograms.

properties.name: management.otlp.metrics.export.max-scale
properties.description: Max scale to use for exponential histograms, if configured.

properties.name: management.otlp.metrics.export.meter
properties.description: Per-meter properties that can be used to override defaults.

properties.name: management.otlp.metrics.export.read-timeout
properties.description: Read timeout for requests to this backend.

properties.name: management.otlp.metrics.export.step
properties.description: Step size (i.e. reporting frequency) to use.

properties.name: management.otlp.metrics.export.url
properties.description: URI of the OTLP server.

properties.name: management.prometheus.metrics.export.descriptions
properties.description: Whether to enable publishing descriptions as part of the scrape payload to Prometheus. Turn this off to minimize the amount of data sent on each scrape.

properties.name: management.prometheus.metrics.export.enabled
properties.description: Whether exporting of metrics to this backend is enabled.

properties.name: management.prometheus.metrics.export.properties
properties.description: Additional properties to pass to the Prometheus client.

properties.name: management.prometheus.metrics.export.pushgateway.address
properties.description: Address (host:port) for the Pushgateway.

properties.name: management.prometheus.metrics.export.pushgateway.enabled
properties.description: Enable publishing over a Prometheus Pushgateway.

properties.name: management.prometheus.metrics.export.pushgateway.format
properties.description: Format to use when pushing metrics.

properties.name: management.prometheus.metrics.export.pushgateway.grouping-key
properties.description: Grouping key for the pushed metrics.

properties.name: management.prometheus.metrics.export.pushgateway.job
properties.description: Job identifier for this application instance.

properties.name: management.prometheus.metrics.export.pushgateway.password
properties.description: Login password of the Prometheus Pushgateway.

properties.name: management.prometheus.metrics.export.pushgateway.push-rate
properties.description: Frequency with which to push metrics.

properties.name: management.prometheus.metrics.export.pushgateway.scheme
properties.description: Scheme to use when pushing metrics.

properties.name: management.prometheus.metrics.export.pushgateway.shutdown-operation
properties.description: Operation that should be performed on shutdown.

properties.name: management.prometheus.metrics.export.pushgateway.token
properties.description: Token to use for authentication with the Prometheus Pushgateway.

properties.name: management.prometheus.metrics.export.pushgateway.username
properties.description: Login user of the Prometheus Pushgateway.

properties.name: management.prometheus.metrics.export.step
properties.description: Step size (i.e. reporting frequency) to use.

properties.name: management.simple.metrics.export.enabled
properties.description: Whether exporting of metrics to this backend is enabled.

properties.name: management.simple.metrics.export.mode
properties.description: Counting mode.

properties.name: management.simple.metrics.export.step
properties.description: Step size (i.e. reporting frequency) to use.

properties.name: management.stackdriver.metrics.export.auto-create-metric-descriptors
properties.description: Whether it should be attempted to create a metric descriptor before writing a time series.

properties.name: management.stackdriver.metrics.export.batch-size
properties.description: Number of measurements per request to use for this backend. If more measurements are found, then multiple requests will be made.

properties.name: management.stackdriver.metrics.export.connect-timeout
properties.description: Connection timeout for requests to this backend.

properties.name: management.stackdriver.metrics.export.enabled
properties.description: Whether exporting of metrics to this backend is enabled.

properties.name: management.stackdriver.metrics.export.metric-type-prefix
properties.description: Prefix for metric type. Valid prefixes are described in the Google Cloud documentation (https://cloud.google.com/monitoring/custom-metrics#identifier).

properties.name: management.stackdriver.metrics.export.project-id
properties.description: Identifier of the Google Cloud project to monitor.

properties.name: management.stackdriver.metrics.export.read-timeout
properties.description: Read timeout for requests to this backend.

properties.name: management.stackdriver.metrics.export.resource-labels
properties.description: Monitored resource's labels.

properties.name: management.stackdriver.metrics.export.resource-type
properties.description: Monitored resource type.

properties.name: management.stackdriver.metrics.export.step
properties.description: Step size (i.e. reporting frequency) to use.

properties.name: management.stackdriver.metrics.export.use-semantic-metric-types
properties.description: Whether to use semantically correct metric types. When false, counter metrics are published as the GAUGE MetricKind. When true, counter metrics are published as the CUMULATIVE MetricKind.

properties.name: management.statsd.metrics.export.buffered
properties.description: Whether measurements should be buffered before sending to the StatsD server.

properties.name: management.statsd.metrics.export.enabled
properties.description: Whether exporting of metrics to StatsD is enabled.

properties.name: management.statsd.metrics.export.flavor
properties.description: StatsD line protocol to use.

properties.name: management.statsd.metrics.export.host
properties.description: Host of the StatsD server to receive exported metrics.

properties.name: management.statsd.metrics.export.max-packet-length
properties.description: Total length of a single payload should be kept within your network's MTU.

properties.name: management.statsd.metrics.export.polling-frequency
properties.description: How often gauges will be polled. When a gauge is polled, its value is recalculated and if the value has changed (or publishUnchangedMeters is true), it is sent to the StatsD server.

properties.name: management.statsd.metrics.export.port
properties.description: Port of the StatsD server to receive exported metrics.

properties.name: management.statsd.metrics.export.protocol
properties.description: Protocol of the StatsD server to receive exported metrics.

properties.name: management.statsd.metrics.export.publish-unchanged-meters
properties.description: Whether to send unchanged meters to the StatsD server.

properties.name: management.statsd.metrics.export.step
properties.description: Step size to use in computing windowed statistics like max. To get the most out of these statistics, align the step interval to be close to your scrape interval.

properties.name: management.metrics.binders.files.enabled
properties.description: Whether to enable files metrics.

properties.name: management.metrics.binders.jvm.enabled
properties.description: Whether to enable JVM metrics.

deprecation.reason: Instead, disable JvmMetricsAutoConfiguration or filter 'jvm' metrics.

properties.name: management.metrics.binders.logback.enabled
properties.description: Whether to enable Logback metrics.

deprecation.reason: Instead, disable LogbackMetricsAutoConfiguration or filter 'logback' metrics.

properties.name: management.metrics.binders.processor.enabled
properties.description: Whether to enable processor metrics.

properties.name: management.metrics.binders.uptime.enabled
properties.description: Whether to enable uptime metrics.

deprecation.reason: No longer supported by the Prometheus client.

properties.name: management.metrics.web.client.request.autotime.enabled
properties.description: Whether to automatically time web client requests.

properties.name: management.metrics.web.client.request.autotime.percentiles
properties.description: Computed non-aggregable percentiles to publish.

properties.name: management.metrics.web.client.request.autotime.percentiles-histogram
properties.description: Whether percentile histograms should be published.

properties.name: management.metrics.web.server.request.autotime.enabled
properties.description: Whether to automatically time web server requests.

properties.name: management.metrics.web.server.request.autotime.percentiles
properties.description: Computed non-aggregable percentiles to publish.

properties.name: management.metrics.web.server.request.autotime.percentiles-histogram
properties.description: Whether percentile histograms should be published.

deprecation.reason: Not needed anymore, direct instrumentation in Spring MVC.
